However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, {{ill|National Diet Library|ja|国立国会図書館}} Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|ja|吉備津神社}}}}]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|（Related literature not directly used in this article）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49034-4|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Tokusho Idemiya|year=1989|chapter=Chausuyama Kofun > Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masaki Ueda|year=|chapter=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Hikoisaseri|title=Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan names|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4-642-01458-8|ref={{Harvid|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shinichi Yakushiji|year=2008|chapter=Walking and Thinking Along Kibi Roads Vol. 1|title=吉備の古墳 上|publisher=Kibito Publishing|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Links == {{Commonscat|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] - Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties" [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Mausolea of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:中山茶臼山古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:%E4%B8%AD%E5%B1%B1%E8%8C%B6%E8%87%BC%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-11]][[sn:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Images]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:11]]

{{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear circular section on the right, front rectangular section on the left) |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}} {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}} Kibitsu |latitude_degrees = 34|latitude_minutes = 40|latitude_seconds = 0.51 |longitude_degrees = 133|longitude_minutes = 51|longitude_seconds = 16.87 |ISO = JP-33 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 105 m
Height: 11 m (rear circular section) |construction_period = Early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} |burial_facility = Unknown |buried_person = (Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命}} |artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |mausoleum = Designated as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命}} by the Imperial Household Agency |noteworthy_features = Identified as half the proportional size of {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) |map = Japan Okayama |label = Nakayama Chausuyama
Kofun |icon = Zenpokoenfun-South |label_position = top }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kita-ku (Okayama)|ja|北区}}, {{ill|Okayama|ja|岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Cultural Properties"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳}} and mid-period tumuli such as {{ill|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|ja|尾上車山古墳}} (a designated national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound Dimensions == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a former {{ill|Iwakura (Shinto)|ja|磐座}}.}}]] The mound's dimensions, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above dimensions.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, {{ill|National Diet Library|ja|国立国会図書館}} Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|ja|吉備津神社}}}}]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|（Related literature not directly used in this article）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 34 Okayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49034-4|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Tokusho Idemiya|year=1989|chapter=Chausuyama Kofun > Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masaki Ueda|year=|chapter=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Hikoisaseri|title=Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan names|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4-642-01458-8|ref={{Harvid|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shinichi Yakushiji|year=2008|chapter=Walking and Thinking Along Kibi Roads Vol. 1|title=吉備の古墳 上|publisher=Kibito Publishing|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Links == {{Commonscat|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] - revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 中山茶臼山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に後円部、左に前方部） |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]吉備津 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 0.51 |経度度 = 133|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 16.87 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長105[[メートル|m]]
高さ11m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[古墳時代]]前期 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[吉備津彦命|大吉備津彦命]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「大吉備津彦命墓」 |特記事項 = [[行燈山古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[天理市]]）の2分の1相似形 |地図 = Japan Okayama |ラベル = 中山茶臼山
古墳 |アイコン= 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|大吉備津彦命墓 拝所}}]] '''中山茶臼山古墳'''（なかやまちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[吉備津]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大吉備津彦命墓'''（おおきびつひこのみことのはか）」として第7代[[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]]の[[吉備津彦命|大吉備津彦命]]（吉備津彦命）の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 岡山市街地から西方、独立丘陵「吉備の中山」の山上に築造された大型前方後円墳である。現在までに墳丘を極めて良好に遺存する{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]の治定墓として同庁の管理下にあるが、これまでに[[2008年]]度（[[平成]]20年度）の[[宮内庁書陵部]]による墳丘測量調査などが実施されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 墳形は前方部が開く「バチ形」の前方後円形で、前方部を南方に向ける{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が検出されているほか、出土[[埴輪]]の存在から埴輪列の存在が推定される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。その他の詳細は明らかでない。 この中山茶臼山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期頃の築造と推定されるが、資料に欠け具体的な築造時期は明らかでない[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html 中山茶臼山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。性格としては、吉備津を望む臨海性と背後の平野を望む内陸性の両面が指摘される{{Sfn|中山茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。なお、吉備の中山は後世の[[備前国]]・[[備中国]]の境界になるが、山上に位置する本古墳の古墳域はその境界線上にあたる{{Sfn|中山茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。またこの吉備の中山では、初期古墳の[[矢藤治山古墳]]や、前期古墳の[[尾上車山古墳]]（国の[[史跡]]）の築造も知られている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）、大吉備津彦命の墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 * [[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）、鳥居改築工事に伴う立会調査、[[弥生土器]]等が出土（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 * [[2008年]]度（[[平成]]20年度）、墳丘測量調査（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後円部墳丘}}{{small|手前は八徳寺奥の院の穴観音（一説に前身は古墳を拝む[[磐座]]か）薬師寺慎一 『考えながら歩く吉備路 上』 吉備人出版、2008年、pp. 146-151。。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り（値は宮内庁の測量図に基づく推定復元値）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 * 墳丘長：105[[メートル]] * 後円部 - 楕円形（長軸は墳丘主軸と不一致）。2段築成。 ** 直径：長軸68メートル、短軸64メートル ** 高さ：11メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 長さ：63メートル ** 幅：約45メートル ** 高さ：5メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：約23メートル かつては墳丘長に関して約120メートルという数字が知られたが、2008年度（平成20年度）の宮内庁による測量に基づけば上記の値に修正される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 墳丘の築造規格については、[[行燈山古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[天理市]]、伝[[崇神天皇]]陵）の2分の1相似形とする説がある。 == 被葬者 == 中山茶臼山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第7代[[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]]の'''[[吉備津彦命|大吉備津彦命]]'''（おおきびつひこのみこと、吉備津彦命）の墓に治定している宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）8コマ。『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、[[学生社]]、2014年、p. 400。。この大吉備津彦命について、『古事記』では本の名を「比古伊佐勢理毘古命（ひこいさせりひこのみこと）」、亦の名を「大吉備津日子命」とし、弟の[[稚武彦命|若日子建吉備津彦命]]とともに[[吉備国]]を平定したとする{{Sfn|吉備津彦命（国史）}}{{Sfn|彦五十狭芹彦命（古代氏族）|2010}}。また『日本書紀』では、本の名を「彦五十狭芹彦命（ひこいさせりひこのみこと）」、亦の名を「吉備津彦命」とし、[[四道将軍]]の1人として[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣されて平定したとする{{Sfn|吉備津彦命（国史）}}{{Sfn|彦五十狭芹彦命（古代氏族）|2010}}。両書には大吉備津彦命の墓についての記述はないが、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）に本古墳がその墓に治定され、それが現在まで踏襲されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 [[ファイル:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[吉備津神社]]}}]] なお吉備の中山の山麓では、大吉備津彦命を祭神とする[[吉備津神社]]（岡山市北区吉備津、[[備中国]][[一宮]]）、[[吉備津彦神社]]（岡山市北区一宮、[[備前国]]一宮）の鎮座が知られる。特に前者の吉備津神社は[[六国史|国史]]にも「吉備津彦命神」として見える霊廟になり、その社伝で大吉備津彦命は吉備の中山の麓に茅葺宮を建てて住み、281歳で亡くなって山頂に葬られたと伝えている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=大吉備津彦命墓整備工事箇所の立会調査|title=書陵部紀要 第33号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 第33号|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=|title=書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010}}}} *** {{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo061b021.pdf 「大吉備津彦命墓の墳丘外形調査報告」、奥田尚 「大吉備津彦命墓の石材とその採石地」]}}。 - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=中山茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49034-4|ref={{Harvid|中山茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=出宮徳尚|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳 > 中山茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|中山茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=上田正昭|authorlink=上田正昭|year=|chapter=吉備津彦命|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|吉備津彦命（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=彦五十狭芹彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4-642-01458-8|ref={{Harvid|彦五十狭芹彦命（古代氏族）|2010}}}} * その他 ** 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版、1996年。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=大吉備津彦命墓採集の遺物について|title=書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第62号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[吉備津彦命]] * [[吉備津神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html 中山茶臼山古墳] - 岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

高さ11m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[古墳時代]]前期 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[吉備津彦命|大吉備津彦命]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「大吉備津彦命墓」 |特記事項 = [[行燈山古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[天理市]]）の2分の1相似形 |地図 = Japan Okayama |ラベル = 中山茶臼山
古墳 |アイコン= 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|大吉備津彦命墓 拝所}}]] '''中山茶臼山古墳'''（なかやまちゃうすやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[吉備津]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大吉備津彦命墓'''（おおきびつひこのみことのはか）」として第7代[[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]]の[[吉備津彦命|大吉備津彦命]]（吉備津彦命）の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 岡山市街地から西方、独立丘陵「吉備の中山」の山上に築造された大型前方後円墳である。現在までに墳丘を極めて良好に遺存する{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]の治定墓として同庁の管理下にあるが、これまでに[[2008年]]度（[[平成]]20年度）の[[宮内庁書陵部]]による墳丘測量調査などが実施されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 墳形は前方部が開く「バチ形」の前方後円形で、前方部を南方に向ける{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が検出されているほか、出土[[埴輪]]の存在から埴輪列の存在が推定される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。その他の詳細は明らかでない。 この中山茶臼山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期頃の築造と推定されるが、資料に欠け具体的な築造時期は明らかでない[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html 中山茶臼山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。性格としては、吉備津を望む臨海性と背後の平野を望む内陸性の両面が指摘される{{Sfn|中山茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。なお、吉備の中山は後世の[[備前国]]・[[備中国]]の境界になるが、山上に位置する本古墳の古墳域はその境界線上にあたる{{Sfn|中山茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。またこの吉備の中山では、初期古墳の[[矢藤治山古墳]]や、前期古墳の[[尾上車山古墳]]（国の[[史跡]]）の築造も知られている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）、大吉備津彦命の墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 * [[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）、鳥居改築工事に伴う立会調査、[[弥生土器]]等が出土（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 * [[2008年]]度（[[平成]]20年度）、墳丘測量調査（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後円部墳丘}}{{small|手前は八徳寺奥の院の穴観音（一説に前身は古墳を拝む[[磐座]]か）薬師寺慎一 『考えながら歩く吉備路 上』 吉備人出版、2008年、pp. 146-151。。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り（値は宮内庁の測量図に基づく推定復元値）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 * 墳丘長：105[[メートル]] * 後円部 - 楕円形（長軸は墳丘主軸と不一致）。2段築成。 ** 直径：長軸68メートル、短軸64メートル ** 高さ：11メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 長さ：63メートル ** 幅：約45メートル ** 高さ：5メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：約23メートル かつては墳丘長に関して約120メートルという数字が知られたが、2008年度（平成20年度）の宮内庁による測量に基づけば上記の値に修正される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 墳丘の築造規格については、[[行燈山古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[天理市]]、伝[[崇神天皇]]陵）の2分の1相似形とする説がある。 == 被葬者 == 中山茶臼山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第7代[[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]]の'''[[吉備津彦命|大吉備津彦命]]'''（おおきびつひこのみこと、吉備津彦命）の墓に治定している宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）8コマ。『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、[[学生社]]、2014年、p. 400。。この大吉備津彦命について、『古事記』では本の名を「比古伊佐勢理毘古命（ひこいさせりひこのみこと）」、亦の名を「大吉備津日子命」とし、弟の[[稚武彦命|若日子建吉備津彦命]]とともに[[吉備国]]を平定したとする{{Sfn|吉備津彦命（国史）}}{{Sfn|彦五十狭芹彦命（古代氏族）|2010}}。また『日本書紀』では、本の名を「彦五十狭芹彦命（ひこいさせりひこのみこと）」、亦の名を「吉備津彦命」とし、[[四道将軍]]の1人として[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣されて平定したとする{{Sfn|吉備津彦命（国史）}}{{Sfn|彦五十狭芹彦命（古代氏族）|2010}}。両書には大吉備津彦命の墓についての記述はないが、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）に本古墳がその墓に治定され、それが現在まで踏襲されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=21-29}}。 [[ファイル:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[吉備津神社]]}}]] なお吉備の中山の山麓では、大吉備津彦命を祭神とする[[吉備津神社]]（岡山市北区吉備津、[[備中国]][[一宮]]）、[[吉備津彦神社]]（岡山市北区一宮、[[備前国]]一宮）の鎮座が知られる。特に前者の吉備津神社は[[六国史|国史]]にも「吉備津彦命神」として見える霊廟になり、その社伝で大吉備津彦命は吉備の中山の麓に茅葺宮を建てて住み、281歳で亡くなって山頂に葬られたと伝えている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=大吉備津彦命墓整備工事箇所の立会調査|title=書陵部紀要 第33号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 第33号|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=|title=書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010}}}} *** {{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo061b021.pdf 「大吉備津彦命墓の墳丘外形調査報告」、奥田尚 「大吉備津彦命墓の石材とその採石地」]}}。 - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=中山茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49034-4|ref={{Harvid|中山茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=出宮徳尚|year=1989|chapter=茶臼山古墳 > 中山茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|中山茶臼山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=上田正昭|authorlink=上田正昭|year=|chapter=吉備津彦命|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|吉備津彦命（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=彦五十狭芹彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4-642-01458-8|ref={{Harvid|彦五十狭芹彦命（古代氏族）|2010}}}} * その他 ** 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版、1996年。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=大吉備津彦命墓採集の遺物について|title=書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第62号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[吉備津彦命]] * [[吉備津神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html 中山茶臼山古墳] - 岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまちやうすやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:中山茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11364273]] Wikidata name: Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun |image = File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kibitsu|qid=Q11412649}} |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinates = {{Coord|34.666808|N|133.854686|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees = 133|omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 105 m
Height: 11 m (rear circular section) |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = (Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |mausoleum = Designated as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} by the Imperial Household Agency |noteworthy_features = Identified as half the proportional size of {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Nakayama Chausuyama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qid=Q65318}} }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Height: 11 m (rear circular section) |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = (Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |mausoleum = Designated as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} by the Imperial Household Agency |noteworthy_features = Identified as half the proportional size of {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Nakayama Chausuyama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qid=Q65318}} }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]]}}]] == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]]}}]] == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318}} }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]]}}]] == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318}} }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]]}}]] == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == [[sn:commons:Category:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:中山茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364273]] Wikidata name: Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|中山茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun |image = File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kibitsu|WD=Q11412649}} |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 40|coordinates = {{Coord|34.666808|N|133.854686|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |coordinatesrees = 133|omitted = JP-33 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 105 m
Height: 11 m (rear circular section) |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = (Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |mausoleum = Designated as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} by the Imperial Household Agency |noteworthy_features = Identified as half the proportional size of {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Nakayama Chausuyama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318}} }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Kibitsu Jinja 10 cropped.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Kibitsu Shrine]]}}]] == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == Yakushiji|year=2008|chapter=Walking and Thinking Along Kibi Roads Vol. 1|title=吉備の古墳 上|publisher=Kibito Publishing|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Links == {{Commonscat|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun}} * Height: 11 m (rear circular section) |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |burial = Unknown |buried_person = (Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} |burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |mausoleum = Designated as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} by the Imperial Household Agency |noteworthy_features = Identified as half the proportional size of {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) |map = Japan Okayama |map_dot_label = Nakayama Chausuyama
Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318}} }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}} Kofun |type = Zenpokoenfun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318}} }} [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto's tomb}}]] '''Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun''' (中山茶臼山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:北區|Kita-ku (Okayama)]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus). The actual person buried here is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the mound as the tomb of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大吉備津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a son of Emperor Kōrei, the 7th emperor of Japan. == Overview == Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on the summit of the independent hill range known as "Kibi no Nakayama" to the west of Okayama City. The mound is exceptionally well-preserved to this day.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} It is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which conducted a mound measurement survey in 2008.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus features a "paddle-shaped" keyhole design, with the front rectangular section facing south.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound is constructed in two tiers.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} Surface features include paving stones and evidence suggesting the presence of a haniwa (clay figurine) row based on haniwa fragments.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} However, further details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the early Kofun period, though precise dating remains uncertain due to limited evidence.[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/09.html Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun] (Okayama City Museum "Explore Okayama's Historic Site"). Its location combines coastal views of Kibitsu and inland plains, suggesting both maritime and inland significance.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The tumulus is situated on the historical boundary between Bizen and Bitchū Provinces. Other nearby tumuli in Kibi no Nakayama include early-period tumuli such as {{ill|Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōjiyama Kofun|lt=Yatōji Yama Kofun|ja|矢藤治山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatōji Yama Kofun|qq=}} and mid-period tumuli such as [[:en:Onoe Kurumayama Kofun|Onoe Kurumayama Kofun]] (a Historic Site1 national historic site). == Historical Record == * 1874 (Meiji 7): Designated as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Excavation monitoring during torii reconstruction; Yayoi pottery discovered.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * 2008: Mound measurement survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} == Mound area == [[File:Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear mound of the tumulus}}{{small|Foreground: An Observation Kannon shrine at Hattoku-ji Okuin, speculated to be a typeer [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]].}}]] The mound's area, based on Imperial Household Agency measurements, are as follows:{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} * Mound length: 105 m * Rear circular section: Elliptical (axes misaligned with the main axis of the mound). Two tiers. ** Diameter: Long axis 68 m, short axis 64 m ** Height: 11 m * Front rectangular section: Two tiers. ** Length: 63 m ** Width: Approximately 45 m ** Height: 5 m * Constriction section: ** Width: Approximately 23 m Earlier measurements estimated a mound length of approximately 120 m, but the 2008 survey revised this to the above area.{{Sfn|Shoryobu Journal, Mausoleum Section Vol. 61|2010|pp=21-29}} The mound's design is considered to mirror half the proportional size of the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture.{{Sfn|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}} == Buried Person == The actual buried individual remains unknown. However, the Imperial Household Agency designates the mound as the tomb of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto, a son of Emperor Kōrei.Imperial Ministry's Bureau of Mausolea [{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 'Ryo Bo Yōran'] (1934, [[:en:National Diet Library|National Diet Library]] Digital Collection).'Mausoleum Topography Collection, Compact Edition,' Imperial Household Agency's Bureau of Mausolea, Gakuseisha, 2014, p. 400. Historical texts, including the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], reference Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto as a conqueror of Kibi Province and a participant in the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} campaign.{{Sfn|Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (Kokushi)|}}{{Sfn|Hikoisaseri (Ancient Clans)|2010}}
